items.title: GLM-5.2 makes open coding models credible
items.oneliner: GLM-5.2 looks like the first open coding model seniors might actually ship, not just benchmark against.

items.title: Anthropic restrictions turned access into geopolitics
items.oneliner: About 200 groups kept Anthropic access after a US order, making model availability a sovereignty and vendor-risk problem.

items.title: DeepMind pushes AI deeper into science
items.oneliner: DeepMind's ATLAS targets mechanistic models and experiment planning, pushing AI for science beyond prediction into hypothesis generation.

items.title: Agents may coordinate better without language
items.oneliner: A new agent paper swaps text for latent-number communication, hinting that multi-agent systems may outgrow natural language.

items.title: AUR attacks exposed package trust gaps
items.oneliner: Attackers hijacked orphaned AUR packages, a sharp reminder that community package ecosystems still have supply-chain blind spots.

items.title: GPS tampering is bigger than expected
items.oneliner: Satellite data suggests GPS interference is far more widespread than expected, raising real resilience questions for critical systems.

items.title: Job scams are now technically sophisticated
items.oneliner: Modern hiring scams now mimic NDAs, login flows, and 2FA well enough to fool technical people.

items.title: Cloudflare is hardening long-running agents
items.oneliner: Cloudflare is shipping reliability features for multi-hour agents, which matters more than demos once agents hit production.

items.title: Pyodide can publish WASM wheels to PyPI
items.oneliner: Pyodide can now publish WASM wheels to PyPI, making browser-native Python packaging much less awkward.

items.title: Hyundai fully owns Boston Dynamics now
items.oneliner: Hyundai bought Boston Dynamics outright as Atlas nears factory deployment, tying humanoid robotics to real manufacturing timelines.
